books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.desc: Tiny changes, remarkable results. A groundbreaking guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, incremental improvements.
books.longDesc: No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving every day. James Clear reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results. If you're having trouble changing your habits, the problem isn't you — the problem is your system.

books.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
books.author: Daniel Kahneman
books.desc: A landmark book exploring the two systems of the mind — the fast, intuitive System 1 and the slow, deliberate System 2.
books.longDesc: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman explains the two systems that drive the way we think. System 1 is fast, intuitive, and emotional; System 2 is slower, more deliberative, and more logical.

books.title: The Lean Startup
books.author: Eric Ries
books.desc: How today's entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create radically successful businesses through validated learning.
books.longDesc: Most startups fail. But many of those failures are preventable. The Lean Startup is a new approach being adopted across the globe, changing the way companies are built and new products are launched.

books.title: Sapiens
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.desc: A brief history of humankind — from the Stone Age to the 21st century, exploring how biology and history have defined us.
books.longDesc: 100,000 years ago, at least six human species inhabited the earth. Today there is just one. Harari examines how we came to believe in gods, nations and human rights, and how we came to be enslaved by bureaucracy and consumerism.

books.title: Deep Work
books.author: Cal Newport
books.desc: Rules for focused success in a distracted world — the ability to focus without distraction is becoming increasingly rare and valuable.
books.longDesc: Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task. Cal Newport shows how deep work is like a superpower and provides a rigorous training regimen for cultivating this skill.

books.title: Man's Search for Meaning
books.author: Viktor E. Frankl
books.desc: A psychiatrist's memoir of surviving Nazi death camps and his discovery of logotherapy — the search for meaning as the primary human drive.
books.longDesc: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl's memoir has riveted generations of readers. Based on his own experience, Frankl argues that we cannot avoid suffering but we can choose how to cope with it, find meaning in it, and move forward with renewed purpose.

books.title: Zero to One
books.author: Peter Thiel
books.desc: Notes on startups, or how to build the future. Every moment in business happens only once.
books.longDesc: Peter Thiel shows how we can find singular ways to create new things. He begins with the contrarian premise that we live in an age of technological stagnation, and charts a path toward genuine progress and abundance.

books.title: The Power of Now
books.author: Eckhart Tolle
books.desc: A guide to spiritual enlightenment, teaching the importance of living in the present moment.
books.longDesc: The Power of Now is a guide to spiritual enlightenment. Tolle shows that our unhappiness is caused by our identification with our minds, and provides practical tools to break free from compulsive thinking.

books.title: Steve Jobs
books.author: Walter Isaacson
books.desc: The exclusive biography based on over forty interviews with Steve Jobs — an intimate portrait of the creative entrepreneur.
books.longDesc: Walter Isaacson has written a riveting story of the roller-coaster life and searingly intense personality of a creative entrepreneur whose passion for perfection revolutionized six industries.

books.title: The 7 Habits
books.author: Stephen Covey
books.desc: Highly effective people share a commitment to principles — a framework for personal and professional effectiveness.
books.longDesc: Covey reveals a step-by-step pathway for living with fairness, integrity, honesty, and human dignity — principles that give us the security to adapt to change and the wisdom to take advantage of new opportunities.

books.title: Influence
books.author: Robert Cialdini
books.desc: The classic book on persuasion, explaining the six principles of influence and how they shape our decisions.
books.longDesc: Dr. Robert Cialdini explains the psychology of why people say yes. You'll learn the six universal principles of influence, how to use them to become a skilled persuader — and how to defend yourself against them.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.desc: An accessible account of cosmology, time, and the universe — a landmark work that made complex physics comprehensible.
books.longDesc: Starting from basic ideas of space and time, Stephen Hawking explains black holes, the big bang, the nature of time, and a unification of physics — all written with remarkable clarity for the non-scientist.
